ops.insert: Explainer Video Script — Absolute Defence (TrackTik-style, Partner Positioning)
90 seconds. Same length as TrackTik's. Long enough to land the message, short enough to send Andy in a follow-up email.
(1) Open with a punchy business problem (2) Frame it as cost / risk / lost trust (3) Position platform as the answer (4) Tie features to outcomes (5) Close with confidence and a CTA

TrackTik solves chaos in security operations. We solve the gap nobody is solving — what happens to the PEOPLE on the premises when an incident is unfolding. We do NOT compete with TrackTik. We are the layer that sits ON TOP.
COOs and security directors at established firms (Dardan, Securitas, Mitie, G4S). They already have ops platforms. They need a way to differentiate, retain contracts, and answer Martyn's Law.
Three business outcomes we tie everything to:
(1) WIN MORE CONTRACTS (2) PROTECT CONTRACT VALUE (3) NEW REVENUE LINE

Aerial drone shot of a corporate campus at dusk. Lights flicker on. A single guard walks the perimeter. Cut to a frightened receptionist alone at a front desk. Cut to a contractor in an empty stairwell. Cut to a cleaner in a basement corridor.
"Your guards are trained. Your operations are tracked. Your patrols are logged."
"But there are sixty other people in your client's building tonight. And when something happens to one of them — your system has nothing for them."
🎬 SCENE 2 (0:10–0:25) — THE PROBLEM (Cost / Risk / Lost Trust)
Quick cuts. Newspaper headlines: '£18M Martyn's Law fines.' '41,226 NHS staff assaults.' 'Corporate manslaughter charges filed.' Each one stamps onto screen with a heavy thud.
"Last year, 642,000 workplace violence incidents happened in the UK. Your guards saw a fraction of them. Your platform recorded fewer."
"Every unreported incident is a contract you can't defend. Every untrained response is a courtroom you can't win. Every silent victim is a client you'll lose."
This is the equivalent of TrackTik's 'chaos is killing security margins.' We are saying: invisible incidents are killing security CONTRACTS.
🎬 SCENE 3 (0:25–0:40) — THE CATEGORY (We Are The Missing Layer)

Animated three-layer stack rises from the ground. Bottom layer labelled 'Guard Operations.' Middle layer labelled 'Workforce Management.' Top layer — glowing cyan — labelled 'Human Protection.' The Absolute Defence logo settles on the top layer.
"Your workforce platform tracks your guards. We protect everybody else."
"Every visitor. Every staff member. Every contractor. Every member of the public on the premises."
"Absolute Defence is the human protection layer that sits on top of your existing operations. Not instead of. On top of."
Andy hears 'on top of, not instead of' — his TrackTik investment is safe. The threat dissolves. Now he's listening.
🎬 SCENE 4 (0:40–1:05) — FEATURES TIED TO BUSINESS OUTCOMES
Three rapid-fire feature/outcome pairs. Each gets ~8 seconds. Each outcome is a CFO word.
Hand holding phone scans a QR poster on a glass wall. Three options bloom on screen: Report — SOS — Guidance. Cut to a Cambridge-style university courtyard with the same QR live.
"A QR code on every wall turns every visitor, every staff member, every contractor into a sensor in your network. No app. No login. Thirty seconds."
"You walk into renewals with intelligence no other security firm can produce."
Acknowledgement chain UI fires up — incident logged, Safety Lead notified, countdown ticking, escalation triggered. Cut to a courtroom. A folder labelled 'Duty of Care Evidence' lands on the bench.
"Every incident is triaged by AI in seconds. Every response is timestamped. Every escalation is logged. Martyn's Law ready. Courtroom ready. Insurer ready."
"One serious incident without proof of due diligence is a lost contract. We make that loss impossible."
Live threat heatmap pulses across a multi-site dashboard. Zone risk scores update in real time. Cut to a quarterly threat review document with a security firm's logo on the cover.
"Eighteen months of zone-level threat intelligence. Predictive heatmaps. Sector benchmarks. White-labelled. Yours to sell."
"Stop selling guard hours. Start selling protected ecosystems. New margin. Stickier clients. Higher contract values."

Black and white archival-style footage of military operators on the ground (silhouetted, abstract — not literal). Dissolve into modern UI of ARIA agents lighting up. Then quick logos: Cambridge-tier client placeholder, Martyn's Law badge, ISO compliance marks.
"Built by operators with thirty years in hostile environments. Not by software companies. Not by tech founders. By the people who've been in the moment."
"Deployed in forty-eight hours. Integrated with your existing platform. UK-hosted. Regulator-ready."
The 'built by operators' line is for Andy specifically. Sandhurst. Royal Anglian. He buys from people who've earned the right to sell.
Hold on the three layers. The top layer (Absolute Defence) glows brighter. Then full screen: Tagline appears. Detect. Respond. Prove. Logo. URL.
"Absolute Defence protects the people the security business is hired to keep safe."
absolutedefence.com  |  Live demo on request  |  Deploys in 48 hours
🎯 DARDAN MEETING — How To Use This Script
Send the 90-second video to Andy 24 hours BEFORE the meeting. Subject line: 'The layer TrackTik never built — for Dardan.' Pre-frame the conversation.
In the meeting, do NOT play the video. Reference it. 'You'll have seen our short film. The reason we made it short is because the gap is simple to describe — and it's costing the industry contracts.'

Lead with the partnership angle Andy can take to Philip Anthony (CEO): 'We are not asking Dardan to replace TrackTik. We are offering Dardan a layer to sell on top of it. Every site you protect becomes a Dardan-branded technology offering. Sticky clients. Higher contracts.'

The shared military background is the foundation. Lead with respect, not product. Ollie's tours and Andy's Sandhurst command shared language. The video does the product talking. The room does the relationship building.
Voiceover talent: Male, British, 40s, low register. Think Idris Elba calm — not Hollywood thriller, not corporate slick. Authority earned, not performed.
Music: Sparse cinematic score. Single sustained note that builds. No drums until Scene 4. Nothing triumphant — something resolved, inevitable.
Colour grade: Cool dark blues with cyan accent on platform UI. Warm flesh tones on the human shots — the contrast tells the story before the words do.

Production route: NotebookLM can generate a draft using this script. For broadcast quality, brief a freelance motion graphics studio (£3-8k) or use Synthesia / Runway for V1, then upgrade. Pin V1 to investor decks and Andy's email.
Sub-30s cutdown: Chop out Scenes 4-5. Keep Hook, Problem, Category, Close. Use as LinkedIn organic + paid creative. Same script, two assets.
